### Nifty hitting 23,800 resistance on short covering – banks leading hard

Nifty just clawed up to 23,818 midday, sitting right on the 23,800 resistance like it’s daring the shorts to cover. Sensex is up over 560 points to 75,744, a clean 0.75% gain, and the move feels like a relief squeeze more than a fresh breakout. What changed? Easing crude prices and fresh positive noise out of US-Iran talks took the geopolitical heat off. Banks are doing the heavy lifting – HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank both running ~2% higher. That’s textbook short-covering into a key level; the street had been pricing in more tension, and today’s tape is showing the unwind instead. The setup right now Expectations were for choppy trade with crude…

### BTC clawing back from sub-75k on Iran deal headlines – relief or just the first bounce?

Trump drops word on progress toward a US-Iran deal and BTC immediately claws back from those sub-75k lows. Sitting right around 75.4k–76k now after the headline. The street had been pricing in fresh risk-off pressure from the whole Middle East mess. De-escalation talk flips that script fast. Crypto had already been feeling the squeeze, and this is the first real unwind of that positioning we’ve seen in a while. What actually moved the needle here is the relief in broader risk sentiment. When geopolitics stops being an immediate tail-risk story, the bid comes back into BTC and the majors without needing fresh ETF flows or macro data to justify…

### Quiet grind higher into the long weekend – bonds and Iran talk doing the heavy lifting?

Markets are doing that quiet grind higher thing mid-afternoon, and honestly it feels a little surprising given how thin the tape is getting into the long weekend. Dow sitting at 50,734.52 up 0.89%, S&P 500 at 7,491.23 (+0.61%), Nasdaq at 26,414.07 (+0.46%). Not a blow-off move, just steady buying that’s held most of the session gains while bonds ease off and the Iran diplomacy chatter keeps providing a little tailwind. ### Markets closed today – back Tuesday 9:30 ET

Markets are dark today for Memorial Day. No tape, no levels, no options flow to obsess over until Tuesday morning. Equity and derivatives exchanges are closed, so everything sits exactly where Friday left it. Trading kicks back up at the usual 9:30 a.m. ET on the 26th. Most of us with overnight exposure are just letting it ride. No gap risk to manage, no early London moves to check. Some folks are using the day to review setups or just step away from the screens completely. Feels like a built-in reset before the next session. What’s everyone planning to watch when the bell rings Tuesday?

### Dow closes 50,580 but Nasdaq barely moved – breadth issue?

Dow just tagged a fresh session high and closed at 50,579.70. Up 294 points, 0.58%. S&P 500 finished at 7,473.47 for a 27.75-point gain, and Nasdaq added 50.87 to land at 26,343.97. Risk-on tone was there, but the breadth looked lopsided. Dow led, S&P followed modestly, and Nasdaq barely participated. That kind of split usually means the move is more about rotation or short-covering than broad conviction. What I'm watching: Whether Nasdaq can catch up tomorrow or if this turns into another Dow-only grind. Volume on the indices – if it was light, the follow-through risk rises. ### Gold slips to $4512 after Iran keeps uranium on soil — buy the dip or just more pressure?

Gold just printed $4,512.49 and gave up another half percent today. After the Iran supreme leader’s directive to keep uranium enrichment on home soil, any last whiff of de-escalation got snuffed out — yet the metal is sliding instead of catching a bid. Street was leaning into the usual safe-haven script: tensions persist, physical demand should step in, miners get a tailwind. What actually showed up was continued pressure across the whole precious-metals complex.
